t_ch: 抱歉，让各位见笑了，我们继续。
t_en: I apologize for the unfortunate interruption. Let us move on with the ceremony.

t_ch: 在怀炎的请求下，你为了追上云璃而暂时离开了司辰宫。
t_en: At the fervent plea of Huaiyan, you temporarily depart from the Palace of Astrum to pursue Yunli.

t_ch: （不出意外的话，云璃就在这附近吧……）
t_en: (Unless I'm mistaken, Yunli should be around here somewhere...)

t_ch: *吸鼻子的声音*
t_en: *sniffle*

t_ch: 小妹妹怎么闹情绪啦？
t_en: What's wrong, Yunli?

t_ch: （再观察一下）
t_en: (Observe a bit more.)

t_ch: *吸鼻子的声音*
t_en: *sniffle*

t_ch: 别看了，说话！
t_en: Stop watching me, and say something!

t_ch: 小妹妹怎么闹情绪啦？
t_en: What's wrong, Yunli?

t_ch: 哎哟哟，要掉小珍珠了吗？
t_en: Poor thing, are you crying?

t_ch: 你想要哭的话，我可以帮你。
t_en: If you want, I can make you cry, too.

t_ch: 你不会是爷爷派来找我的吧？
t_en: Did Grandpa send you to look for me?

t_ch: 你肯定就是。
t_en: I bet he did.

t_ch: 你都已经学会抢答了。
t_en: Oh, so now you're a mind reader?

t_ch: 其实我是关心你才来的。
t_en: Actually, I'm here because I care about you.

t_ch: 是这样吗？谢谢你。
t_en: Really? Thank you.

t_ch: 先说好，我会守在这里，趁守卫不备把魔剑拦下来，接着在所有人反应过来之前就把它给熔了！你拦不住我的，没人能拦住我。
t_en: Just so you know, I'm here just waiting for the perfect moment to snatch that sword. And once I have it, I'll melt it down before anyone can react! Don't even think about stopping me. No one can stop me.

t_ch: 你要不解释清楚，没人会帮你。
t_en: No one can help you if you don't explain what's going on.

t_ch: …你是爷爷派来的，他可不希望你来帮我。
t_en: ...Grandpa sent you here. Surely he doesn't want you to help me.

t_ch: 你觉得我会允许你继续闯祸？
t_en: You think I'm just going to let you cause more trouble?

t_ch: …你确实有些身手，但是我不希望你这个时候来阻止我。
t_en: ...You do have some skill, but now is not a good time to try to stop me.

t_ch: 那是一柄「魔剑」，你听说过「魔剑」吗？
t_en: Have you ever heard of cursed swords? That sword is one.

t_ch: 有魔力的剑？恶魔的剑？
t_en: So it's a sword that's been cursed?

t_ch: 从字面上来看，这么说也没错。
t_en: I guess that's what it means literally.

t_ch: 传说中失去资格的圣剑？
t_en: So it's like a sacred sword that's been corrupted?

t_ch: 看来你听说过不少传说故事，但我说的「魔剑」并不是那样的。
t_en: You've clearly heard a lot of folk tales, but that's not the kind of "cursed sword" I'm talking about.

t_ch: 所谓魔剑——即便从未受训剑术之人，只要握住剑柄，便有高深奥妙的剑技在脑海中闪现；即便是手无缚鸡之力的弱者，也能以惊人的力量和肉眼难及的速度挥剑。
t_en: A cursed sword lets anyone, even a complete novice, wield it with insane skill. Just by holding the hilt, even the weakest rookie can brandish it with incredible speed and strength.

t_ch: 三月七学剑也许能一天就毕业。
t_en: With something like that, March 7th could graduate in just one day.

t_ch: 那么，在哪能买到这种东西呢？
t_en: So, where can I buy one?

t_ch: 我不是在和你开玩笑，我要说的也不是什么荒诞不经的奇闻……
t_en: I'm not joking around, and I'm not making up some crazy story...

t_ch: 持有魔剑的人虽然能在剑技上一蹴而就，如同饱经苦练的高手一般，但这一切都有代价。
t_en: While a cursed sword grants instant abilities to its wielder, it comes at a cost.

t_ch: 就像是不断往铸炉中添加燃料一般，随着每次挥剑出击，持剑人渐渐会被掌中之剑汲取血髓，日日不竭，油尽灯枯。
t_en: It's like continuously adding fuel to a forge. With each swing, the sword drains the wielder's blood and essence. Day by day, the fuel will run out, leaving nothing but an empty husk.

t_ch: 用不了多久，就不再是由人役使剑，而是人成了一具被剑驱遣的行尸走肉，脑袋里充塞着种种嗜血好杀的念头。
t_en: Soon enough, it will no longer be a person wielding a sword but a mindless killing machine consumed by bloodlust and murderous thoughts.

t_ch: 这情况听起来十分耳熟…
t_en: This sounds oddly familiar...

t_ch: 不知你有没有听说过一种名为「岁阳」的精怪？这东西酷爱汲取人类的情绪与记忆，热衷操控血肉之躯。
t_en: I wonder if you've ever heard of a creature called a "heliobus"? It loves to feed on human emotions and memories, and excels at manipulating its hosts.

t_ch: 就像是被岁阳占据那样？
t_en: So, it's like being possessed by a heliobus?

t_ch: 你听说过岁阳？那样倒好，解释起来也不用多费口舌了。
t_en: Oh, so you're familiar with heliobi? That makes this much easier to explain.

t_ch: 所谓「魔剑」的真相就是如此，有痴于铸剑的匠人，将岁阳铸进了金铁之中，渴望让武器成为有灵之物。
t_en: There was a lunatic swordsmith who infused metal blades with heliobi, as he was obsessed with turning weapons into living things, and this is how cursed swords were made.

t_ch: 所以孤云也是魔剑？
t_en: So, Guyun is one of those cursed swords?

t_ch: 所以你父亲是将岁阳铸进剑中？
t_en: So, your father sealed a heliobus inside that sword?

t_ch: 我知道在「赠剑仪式」上我做得不对。可我仔细思考过了，要是我不闯祸，要不了多久，那把魔剑就该闯祸了——所以还是让我来闯祸吧！
t_en: I know I shouldn't have caused a scene at the Sword-Gifting Ceremony, but I've thought this through. If I don't make a fuss now, that cursed sword will cause a whole lot more trouble. So, it's better if it's me causing the trouble.

t_ch: …对了，他们不是计划把剑送去工造司修缮吗？怎么还没人出来…你有什么头绪吗？
t_en: ...By the way, aren't they supposed to be taking the sword to the Artisanship Commission for repairs? Why haven't they come out yet... Any ideas?

t_ch: 也许司辰宫不止有一个前门…
t_en: Maybe there are other ways out of the Palace of Astrum...

t_ch: 看来在这里是蹲不到了，我得直接去工造司。
t_en: Looks like they're not going to show up here. I'll just head straight to the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 不管爷爷是出于什么原因选中了那柄剑。我都不能让它落入别人的手里。
t_en: It doesn't matter why Grandpa chose that sword, I won't let it fall into anyone else's hands.

t_ch: 我会像我过去做过的那样，一柄一柄地找到那人铸造的剑，将它们一一折断，尽数熔毁！
t_en: I'll track down every single sword he forged and melt them down one after another, just like I've been doing all these years!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，事出紧急，我们先就此别过了。
t_en: I have to go now, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: （听她刚才的话，似乎准备潜入工造司带走那把剑？）
t_en: (Is she planning to sneak into the Artisanship Commission and steal that sword?)

t_ch: （也许还是看着她为好……）
t_en: (Maybe I should keep an eye on her...)

t_ch: （工造司这么大，她会去哪儿呢？）
t_en: (The Artisanship Commission is so big. Where could she be?)

t_ch: 刚才有个小姑娘气势汹汹地向我问路。她说她是工造司的，你认识吗？
t_en: Just a while ago, a girl came up to me, all fierce and demanding directions. She said she's a member of the Artisanship Commission. Do you know her?

t_ch: 我不认识啊！既然是工造司的人，又怎么会不认得这儿的路呢？
t_en: No idea who she is! If she really works here, how can she not know her way around?

t_ch: 我认识，是朱明仙舟工造司的。
t_en: I know her. She's from the Xianzhou Zhuming's Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 哦——这就不奇怪了，这就不奇怪了。
t_en: Oh, I see. That explains it then.

t_ch: 那怎么就不奇怪了？很奇怪好吧！
t_en: Explains what? It explains nothing at all!

t_ch: 你说的姑娘是不是提着大剑？
t_en: Was she carrying a massive sword?

t_ch: 身高没一丁点儿，手里的家伙事倒还挺吓人！
t_en: Yeah, she wasn't big at all, but that weapon of hers was pretty intimidating!

t_ch: 她气势汹汹就往工造司里冲，我也不敢多问啊。
t_en: She was in a hurry, heading straight for the Artisanship Commission, and I didn't want to ask too many questions.

t_ch: 「我是工造司的，你们这里存放贵重物的地方在哪？」我腿一软就给她指了去公输师傅金人仓库的路。
t_en: She said something like, "I'm a member of the Artisanship Commission. Where do you keep your precious stuff?" I got scared and pointed her toward Master Gongshu's warehouse where he stores his aurumatons.

t_ch: 等等，你怎么还给她指路了，万一她是来打劫的呢？
t_en: Wait, why did you give her directions? What if she's planning to steal something?

t_ch: 说重点，她人往哪儿去了？
t_en: Let's cut to the chase. Where did she go?

t_ch: 还能去哪？都说了，她要往存放金人的仓库去了，那里的金人都挺贵重的。
t_en: Where else? Like I said, she was headed straight for the warehouse, where all the valuable aurumatons are.

t_ch: 那地方可全是公输师傅的宝贝金人，等着瞧吧，她得不了好。
t_en: Yeah, that place is filled with Master Gongshu's precious aurumatons. I bet they'll give her a hard time.

t_ch: 那里的机巧守卫严密得很，希望小姑娘人没事。
t_en: The security there is tight. I hope that little girl is okay.

t_ch: 你怎么赶来了，我一个人闯祸就好，不想连累你。
t_en: What are you doing here? Look, if I get into trouble, it's my problem. I don't want to drag you into it.

t_ch: 现在是说这个的时候吗？
t_en: Is this really the time to talk about that?

t_ch: 我答应了你爷爷要看住你。
t_en: I promised your grandpa that I'd keep an eye on you.

t_ch: 那好，我们先砸了这些铁罐子，有话一会再说！
t_en: Fine, let's smash these metal cans first, then we can talk!

t_ch: 终于，全都消停了，多谢你帮忙解围。
t_en: That was the last of them. Thank you for helping me out.

t_ch: 虽然这防备周密…但这除了金人还是金人，存着刀剑的武库又在哪里呢……
t_en: This place is heavily guarded, but... there's nothing but aurumatons. Where is the arsenal...?

t_ch: 看起来为我指路的匠人也不知道武库在哪里，就这么翻找下去，也没有任何效率，说不定我还会被爷爷先带走……
t_en: Seems like not even those craftsmen know where the arsenal is... At this rate, we won't find anything, and Grandpa might take me away before I can do anything...

t_ch: …看来你也不是单纯来帮忙解围，你是来阻止我的，对吧？
t_en: ...You're not really here to help me. You're here to stop me, right?

t_ch: 我当然要阻止，看不得你闯祸。
t_en: Of course I'm here to stop you. I can't just stand there and watch you get into trouble.

t_ch: …那你这人还怪好嘞。
t_en: ...You have a kind soul.

t_ch: 你好归好，能不能阻止我就得看你的本事了。
t_en: But if you want to stop me, you'll have to prove yourself.

t_ch: 这魔剑可不止是你一人的问题。
t_en: You know, this cursed sword isn't just your problem.

t_ch: …是啊，爷爷他老人家一言既出，不仅会在外来使团面前失信，对罗浮这边也不好交代。
t_en: ...No, it's not. Grandpa made a promise to the delegation, and going back on his word would tarnish his reputation and make things difficult for the Luofu.

t_ch: 不过我想，若将魔剑长留在此，也是对罗浮不好交代。权衡之下，不如将魔剑熔了，让我一人来承担。
t_en: But leaving that cursed sword here will also make things difficult for the Luofu. So all things considered, the best outcome is for me to melt that sword down and take all the blame.

t_ch: …你肯定心里在想，云璃这姑娘又冲动又蛮不讲理，对吧？
t_en: ...You probably think I'm being impulsive and unreasonable, right?

t_ch: 我只是感觉你对魔剑过于执着…
t_en: Well, I just feel like you're too obsessed with cursed swords...

t_ch: 有吗？我表现得很执着吗？…好吧，我确实是这样。
t_en: Oh, really? Do I come across that way?... Well, I guess I can't deny it.

t_ch: 现在你有一个讲完谜语的机会。
t_en: Now you have a chance to explain yourself.

t_ch: 看来，你遇到过不少只把话说一半的人。
t_en: It seems like you've met a lot of people who don't tell you the whole story.

t_ch: 我感觉你还有什么难言之隐？
t_en: Looks like this is a sensitive topic?

t_ch: 你很敏锐，你说那是难言之隐也没有错……
t_en: You're sharp. Yeah, you could say it's a sensitive topic...

t_ch: 对啊，你竟然还是有自知之明。
t_en: So you are self-aware after all.

t_ch: 我、我也不是一直这样啊，要不是那魔剑……
t_en: H—Hey! I'm not always like this! It's all because of that cursed sword...

t_ch: 不妨坐下来，跟我好好讲讲…
t_en: Why don't you just tell me everything...?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我欠你一个解释…不过，这儿不是说话的地方，咱们得换个去处。我得提前声明，我可没有消气，这…这只能算是中场休息。
t_en: Look, {NICKNAME}, I owe you an explanation... but this isn't the best place to talk. We need to find somewhere else. Let me be clear though, I'm still pretty riled up. This... this is just a temporary break.

t_ch: 记得我向你解释过，今天赠剑仪式上被送出的孤云，正是一柄被铸入岁阳的魔剑。而铸造它的匠人含光，是我的父亲。
t_en: I already told you about Guyun, the sword that was returned during the ceremony. It's a cursed sword with a heliobus inside. And Hanguang, the swordsmith who forged it... is my father.

t_ch: 除了爷爷，我很少向外人提起「含光」这个名字，就像你猜测的那样——我或许一直在逃避着再去谈论他，可只要我仍在猎取魔剑，我就没法避而不谈……
t_en: I rarely mention his name to anyone other than Grandpa. Maybe, like you said, I've been avoiding having to talk about him. But as long as my mission to hunt down cursed swords continues, it's impossible to avoid the topic forever...

t_ch: 不知为何，面对你，我好像能敞开心扉……
t_en: For some reason, I feel like I can open up to you...

t_ch: 关于含光的许多事情我已经记不清了，只记得当时焰轮铸炼宫往来不绝，异邦旅人踏破门槛，只求一剑，他们争相吹捧着含光为应星之后的运斤大匠。
t_en: Honestly, I don't remember much about him. All I know is that the Flamewheel Forge used to be a bustling place, with people from all over coming to get one of his swords. They called him the greatest master craftsman since Yingxing.

t_ch: 我虽然听不清他们详细交谈了什么，却仍记得含光锻出旷世神兵，求剑的异邦旅人则浮现出笑容——我曾以为匠人是给人带来幸福的职业。
t_en: I didn't catch all the details, but I do remember seeing him forge those amazing swords while the visitors watched with smiles on their faces. I used to believe that craftsmen brought happiness to others.

t_ch: 「自焰轮铸炼宫中所出的剑器蜚声星海，锋芒难当，无往不胜。得先生剑器者，纵使是荒野匹夫，也能削铁如泥，无往不利。」
t_en: "The swords crafted by the Flamewheel Forge are famed across the stars. They possess exceptional sharpness and invincibility. With these legendary weapons, even ordinary people can become skilled warriors, capable of overcoming the most formidable opponents."

t_ch: 含光贪心铸剑匠人的浮名，不停试探着禁忌的边缘，锻造出数百柄不应存世的魔剑，那些欲望扭曲的求剑人更是如蚁附膻，往来不绝。
t_en: But then, he became obsessed with becoming a famed swordsmith and started crossing all sorts of lines. He forged cursed swords that should never exist, and all those people who desired those twisted swords started flooding into the palace.

t_ch: 然而爷爷说过，好剑之人，必伤于剑。
t_en: However, just like Grandpa said, those who excel with the sword will eventually suffer by it.

t_ch: 异邦旅人如同泥沙俱下，求剑而未得之人，得剑而未尝所愿之人，亦或是单纯想巧取豪夺之人…在含光亲手铸造的混沌中，一柄剑锋刺向了他。
t_en: The palace was overrun with visitors from afar. Some left empty-handed, others got swords that didn't fulfill their desires, and some even resorted to stealing. And in the midst of all the chaos my father created, he ended up being stabbed by one of the swords he forged.

t_ch: 这一切是那么猝不及防，就在一瞬间，所有声音都像被滤去一样，只有筋骨碎裂、鲜血泵出的声音被无限放大——
t_en: It was so sudden and unexpected. Everything went silent for a moment. All I could hear was the sound of bones breaking and blood pouring out...

t_ch: 我一步都迈不出去。直到母亲将我从劈砍的魔剑前推开，我才能趔趔趄趄地跑起来。
t_en: I was frozen in place, unable to move. It was only when my mother pushed me away from the deadly swing of the cursed sword that I managed to escape.

t_ch: 再接下来的记忆已经很模糊了，我只记得心跳声、急促的喘气声，我听见焰轮铸炼宫的匠人不断大喊着「云璃快跑」，还有无数的惨叫声。
t_en: I don't remember much after that, except for the pounding of my heart and the sound of my own gasps of breath. I could hear the smiths in the Flamewheel Forge shouting, "Run away, Yunli!" and the screams of agony.

t_ch: 我的眼泪怎么也抹不净，什么也看不清，我越喘越急，再后来我摔倒在一片血泊之中……
t_en: My tears wouldn't stop, and I couldn't see anything. I kept gasping for air until I collapsed in a pool of blood...

t_ch: …若不是怀炎将军及时赶到，我今天也许没法在这里同你对话了。
t_en: ...If General Huaiyan hadn't arrived in time, I probably wouldn't be here talking to you now.

t_ch: 在那之后，怀炎将军收养了我，我俩以爷孙相称。但对我而言，他胜过那个造孽的父亲百倍。爷爷不仅教了我铸艺，还教会了我剑术。
t_en: After that, General Huaiyan took me in and treated me like his own granddaughter. To me, he is a hundred times better than my sinful father. He taught me forging and swordsmanship.

t_ch: 含光自食其果，但他留下的遗患却不能放任。按图谱所记，含光历年铸造的岁阳魔剑共计一千三百零八十二柄，其中形制特殊的计有一百八十二柄。
t_en: Hanguang paid for his sins, but his troubled legacy must not go unchecked. According to the records, he forged a total of <unbreak>1,382</unbreak> heliobus-cursed swords, and <unbreak>182</unbreak> of them had unique designs.

t_ch: 从入焰轮铸炼宫受训开始，我便立誓要猎尽魔剑。立誓至今，我已经猎取了有三百一十二柄。而孤云就是这第三百一十三柄。
t_en: When I joined the Flamewheel Forge for training, I made a vow to hunt down all those cursed swords. So far, I've found <unbreak>312</unbreak> of them, and Guyun will be my <unbreak>313</unbreak>th.

t_ch: 它会像其他魔剑一样——岁阳将被彻底分离，剑身化为铁水被铸进「熔铁剑骸」之中。
t_en: Like all the other cursed swords... I'll separate it from the heliobus, then I'll melt it down and make it part of the "Blade of Forged Remnants."

t_ch: 话已经说完了，虽然有些对不住爷爷…我还是得熔了这把剑。
t_en: I've said my piece now. Even though it'll be tough for Grandpa... I still have to melt down the sword.

t_ch: 不过，使团一直称它英雄之剑…
t_en: But the delegation calls it the "Sword of Heroes"...

t_ch: 那些来向含光求剑的异邦人，没有一个不打着光明正大的旗号。
t_en: Those travelers who came seeking Hanguang's swords all came in the name of justice and honor.

t_ch: 那些大英雄大豪杰里，多的是杀人盈野的刽子手。只因他们是历史的胜利者，便得到了英雄的美称。
t_en: Most of those so-called "great heroes" were heartless killers. They are only called heroes because they won.

t_ch: 那些被我回收毁去的魔剑，其中也有不少有着光鲜响亮的名头。宝剑与屠刀往往是对同一种东西的不同称呼罢了。
t_en: Many of the cursed swords I've destroyed had honorable and grand names too. A legendary sword and a butcher's knife are often just different ways of describing the same thing.

t_ch: 我觉得你最好和怀炎将军聊聊。
t_en: I think you should talk to General Huaiyan about it.

t_ch: 你是说…爷爷知道魔剑一事，却仍然将它选作奖品，也就说明了它没有问题？
t_en: So, you're saying... even though Grandpa knew it was a cursed sword, he still chose it to be a prize because he thought it wouldn't cause any trouble?

t_ch: 不对，怎么想都是爷爷有问题，你还记得吗？关于「赠剑仪式」，他是一点都没有告诉我，他肯定有什么瞒着我。
t_en: No, no matter how I think about it, something is definitely off. Grandpa didn't tell me anything about the ceremony. He must be hiding something.

t_ch: 再说我们俩今天都吵成那样了，要我现在回去找他聊聊？不行，至少今天不行。明天的话，我考虑考虑……
t_en: Besides, after our argument today, you want me to go back and talk to him now? No way, at least not today. Maybe tomorrow...

t_ch: 不知怎么的，和你聊完之后就像让脑袋淬火降温一般，现在我冷静了不少。
t_en: Somehow, after talking to you, I feel like I've cooled off a bit.

t_ch: 这样吧，明天我会找个时间和爷爷开诚布公地聊一聊。在他说出理由前，我暂时不会对孤云出手。
t_en: Well, I'll have an honest conversation with Grandpa tomorrow, and I won't take any action against Guyun until he gives me his reasons.

t_ch: 记得向怀炎将军道歉。
t_en: Don't forget to apologize to General Huaiyan.

t_ch: 公共场合里…是我不对。
t_en: Yeah, I shouldn't have done that in a public place.

t_ch: 无论如何都要谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。我们明天见。
t_en: Anyway, thank you, {NICKNAME}. We'll catch up tomorrow.

t_ch: （至少要将工造司里发生的事告诉怀炎将军，现在回司辰宫一趟吧。）
t_en: (I should go back to the Palace of Astrum and tell General Huaiyan what happened at the Artisanship Commission.)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我那孙女没有惹出什么事吧？
t_en: My granddaughter didn't cause any trouble, did she, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: （向怀炎解释云璃的事情）
t_en: (You explain Yunli's situation to Huaiyan.)

t_ch: 你详细述说了事情经过，怀炎紧张的神色也变得缓和了一些。
t_en: You explain what happened in detail, and Huaiyan's nervous expression eases a little.

t_ch: 让你看笑话了呀。云璃与你讲了不少，不过，你也听听我这老头的说法吧。
t_en: Sorry for all this trouble. Yunli told you quite a bit. Now, it's time for this old man to tell my side of the story.

t_ch: 含光是老朽疼爱的徒弟，也是自应星之后焰轮铸炼宫里罕有的匠作天才。
t_en: Hanguang was my beloved disciple, a rare genius not seen in the Flamewheel Forge since Yingxing.

t_ch: 但天赋有时候也会成为一个人的诅咒。他对铸剑之事有着异于常人的热情，一直渴望打造出超越人智的刀剑，让人无需受训便能成为久历生死的老兵。
t_en: However, talent can sometimes be a curse. He had an unusual fervor for forging, and he dreamed of forging a sword with self-awareness that surpassed that of humans. He believed it would make warriors invincible without any training.

t_ch: 在他看来，以「朱明火」或是「炼石箭」之类的大规模火力消灭敌人固然有效，但只要战争中仍需要士兵接敌鏖战，不畏生死才是左右胜负的关键。
t_en: According to him, while weapons of mass destruction like Zhuming Flames and Alchemical Arrows can destroy many enemies, true victory lies with soldiers who fearlessly fight, ready to lay their lives on the line.

t_ch: 仙舟人铸造剑器时会赋以简单的灵智，便于士兵操纵。但要克服恐惧，培养敏锐的临战判断，仍需要漫长的时间不可，所以——
t_en: Usually, Xianzhou swordsmiths infuse a basic level of awareness into their swords to make them easier to wield. But even then, soldiers need to experience countless battles to overcome fear and sharpen their instincts, so...

t_ch: 他想到用岁阳吸取情感…
t_en: So, he thought of using heliobi to absorb emotions...

t_ch: 他想到用岁阳传递经验…
t_en: So, he thought of using heliobi to impart combat experience...

t_ch: 将岁阳铸进剑中，固然能令武器传递经验，克服恐惧，甚至代替人做出判断。但武器与别的工具不同，它的唯一用途只有杀伤敌人。
t_en: By infusing heliobi into swords, he managed to forge weapons that could grant their wielders strength and valor, and even make decisions for them. However, weapons are different from regular tools. They are meant to kill, plain and simple.

t_ch: 在无数战斗之后，嗜血、嗔怒、畏怖的情绪被剑中的岁阳汲取，握住它的士兵不仅会继承剑技，还会共享杀戮的情绪，成了被剑操控的杀戮傀儡。
t_en: After countless battles, all the anger, fear, and bloodlust are soaked up by the heliobi within the swords. The soldiers wielding them not only gain strength, but they also become consumed by the malice, turning into puppets possessed by the swords.

t_ch: 无论铸剑师怀着怎样美好的初衷，这些饱经杀戮的武器，最终都会沦为魔剑。
t_en: No matter how noble Hanguang's intentions were, those weapons, soaked in blood, turned into cursed swords eventually.

t_ch: 后来，含光在异邦旅人劝诱鼓动下不断铸剑。旅人编撰了各种需要神兵利器的理由——什么凭宝剑光复故国，什么凭仙人之剑斩妖诛邪……
t_en: Later, outworlders got wind of what he was doing and encouraged him to keep forging cursed swords. They came up with all sorts of reasons, from taking back their kingdoms to slaying demons...

t_ch: 而再后来的事你知道了——她的父母相继倒在血泊之中，焰轮铸炼宫的匠人们也多有死伤。云璃虽然侥幸逃过一劫，但她并没有从这段过去中逃出来。
t_en: And you know what happened next. That massacre took her parents' lives and the lives of many craftsmen in the Flamewheel Forge. Yunli managed to survive, but she couldn't escape the horrors of that day.

t_ch: 难怪将军不想让她看到那剑…
t_en: That's why you didn't want her to see that sword...

t_ch: 是啊。我不想云璃这孩子一生都活在她父亲留下的阴影之中…含光的错误，不该由他的女儿来背负。
t_en: Yes, I don't want her to spend her whole life trapped under her father's shadow... She doesn't deserve to carry the weight of his mistakes.

t_ch: 云璃虽然立誓要「搜猎魔剑，尽数断绝」。想完成这个誓言，所付出的代价绝不轻松——仅是跋涉星海寻剑，就无异于海底捞针。
t_en: Despite her vow to hunt down and wipe out all cursed swords, fulfilling that vow requires immense effort. Traversing the starry seas in search of the swords is like finding a needle in a haystack.

t_ch: 而要夺回魔剑，就要同杀孽缠身的剑主决斗，时刻徘徊在生死边缘。她收回魔剑三百余柄，所受过的致命伤也有三百余处。
t_en: And to reclaim those swords, she has to duel the bloodthirsty wielders, teetering on the edge of life and death. She has taken back over three hundred cursed swords and has suffered the same number of fatal wounds.

t_ch: 我收养云璃长大，是想看到她在天真烂漫的年纪做着天真烂漫的事，而不是踏上「猎剑」的旅途。
t_en: I took her in to give her a chance at a normal childhood, not to send her on a sword-hunting journey.

t_ch: 就算有朝一日她熔毁了所有魔剑，到那时她的人生又该去向何方呢？
t_en: Even if she manages to melt down all the cursed swords one day, what will she do with her life after?

t_ch: 那柄剑真的被铸入了岁阳？
t_en: Does that sword really contain a heliobus?

t_ch: 孤云确实是含光铸造的魔剑之一。
t_en: Guyun is indeed one of the cursed swords forged by Hanguang.

t_ch: 很少有匠人能够做到将岁阳作为铸剑的材料，含光便是那少数人之一。他为自己的尝试付出了惨痛的代价……
t_en: Very few swordsmiths could use heliobi as forging material, and Hanguang was one of them. His attempt came at a terrible cost...

t_ch: 云璃打算明天和你聊聊…
t_en: Yunli plans to talk to you tomorrow...

t_ch: 这柄孤云本应该成为一个契机——我会告诉她，她记忆中那个「父亲」并非是一成不变的，就算是她怨恨的含光也能打造出「英雄之剑」。
t_en: I thought this sword could be an opportunity. I wanted to tell her that her father wasn't all evil, that even the man she resents so much managed to forge a true Sword of Heroes.

t_ch: 我也想找个机会，和她好好谈谈这柄剑的下落与经历…但那不应该是「赠剑仪式」，你看得出来，那不是一个好契机。
t_en: I also wanted to find a chance to tell her about the history and origin of this sword... but not during the ceremony. As you saw, it wasn't a good time.

t_ch: 我已吩咐过工造司，将剑存放入武库中好好保管。你若是放心不下，不妨问问相关人士。
t_en: I've instructed the Artisanship Commission to keep the sword safe in the arsenal. If you have any doubts, feel free to ask the people involved.

t_ch: 既然我那孙女说要与我明日对话，我自会等等她。顺便一问，你照顾了她半晌，很累吧？
t_en: Since my granddaughter mentioned talking to me tomorrow, I'll be waiting for her. By the way, you watched over her for quite some time. You must be exhausted, aren't you?

t_ch: 很累！
t_en: Totally exhausted!

t_ch: 美少女的事都不累。
t_en: A pretty lady like her never gets tiring.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，那就多麻烦你了。
t_en: Hahaha, I'll be counting on you then.

t_ch: 云璃就多拜托你照顾了。
t_en: Please take good care of Yunli.

t_ch: （该回去看看逃课的三月七了……）
t_en: (Time to go back and find that class-skipping March 7th...)

t_ch: 忙完了这一切，你回到了列车，见到了逃课被抓包的三月七。
t_en: After finishing it all, you returned to the Express and came face-to-face with March 7th, caught skipping class.

t_ch: 你们喝着奶茶、聊着八卦，时间不经意间流逝。到了第二天……
t_en: Sipping milk tea and gossiping, time slipped away unnoticed. The next day arrived...

t_ch: 好，人都到齐了，该是问话的时候了。
t_en: Alright, everyone is here. It's time for questioning.

t_ch: …云璃，执事官叫你来的原因，想必你也清楚了吧。
t_en: ...Yunli, I'm sure you understand why the official called you here, right?

t_ch: 我知道，剑不是我偷的。
t_en: Yeah, but I didn't steal the sword.

t_ch: 我可以作她的担保人。
t_en: I can vouch for her.

t_ch: 哎，现在可不是你滥用好名声的时候。
t_en: Now is not the time to use your reputation like that.

t_ch: 云璃向我承诺过不会偷剑。
t_en: Yunli promised me she wouldn't steal the sword.

t_ch: 哎，大毫执事官可没有听到云璃的承诺。
t_en: Unfortunately, Official Dahao didn't hear Yunli's promise.

t_ch: 孤云失窃，对内失信于罗浮，对外有负使团。大毫执事官，请务必查清真相。
t_en: If we don't recover the sword, we will be breaking our promise to the Luofu and betraying the delegation. We're counting on you to find the truth, Official Dahao.

t_ch: 是，详加审查是我的分内职责。云璃小姐话虽说得斩钉截铁，但只凭个人陈词是不能结案的。
t_en: Yes, it's my duty to thoroughly investigate the case. Even though Yunli is firm in her words, we can't just rely on her testimony.

t_ch: 与其让歹人偷走，还不如一开始就交给我熔了呢。
t_en: After everything, the sword still got stolen. They should have given it to me from the beginning.

t_ch: 你就少说两句吧…
t_en: Just keep quiet...

t_ch: 就是，就是…
t_en: Yeah, I agree...

t_ch: 孤云被盗一事，虽然所有知情者各有大小不一的嫌疑。但其中云璃小姐是本案最大的嫌疑人。
t_en: In this case of Guyun's theft, everyone involved seems suspicious to some extent, but Miss Yunli is the main suspect.

t_ch: 首先，云璃小姐在赠剑仪式当场就表露过偷取孤云的动机——几次三番说过要带走这把剑。而后又闯入工造司中，破坏金人，意图夺剑，我可有说错？
t_en: First, during the sword-gifting ceremony, Yunli openly expressed her intention to steal the sword. She even mentioned it multiple times. After the ceremony, she broke into the Artisanship Commission and destroyed aurumatons in an attempt to get the sword. Am I right?

t_ch: …没错。
t_en: ...That's true.

t_ch: 地衡司检查了存放孤云的武库，门窗均无破坏痕迹。除宽窄不过数掌的气窗外，没有任何可供窃贼出入的通道。
t_en: The Realm-Keeping Commission inspected the arsenal where Guyun was stored, and there were no signs of forced entry through the doors or windows. The only possible entrance or exit was a small window gap, just a few inches wide.

t_ch: 啊，是密室凶案…
t_en: Ah, it's like a locked-room murder mystery...

t_ch: 喂喂，只是一桩盗窃案而已！
t_en: Hey, hey, it's just a theft!

t_ch: 要是三月七在就好了…
t_en: Wish we had March 7th here...

t_ch: （要是三月七在，靠她的乌鸦嘴一定能替咱们指出谁是窃贼…）
t_en: (If March 7th were here, she'd probably jinx it and accidentally reveal who the thief was...)

t_ch: 装着孤云的剑匣被打开，剑不翼而飞，但同处一室的其他剑却安然无恙，这说明——
t_en: The box containing Guyun was opened, and the sword went missing, but none of the other swords in the room were touched, which means...

t_ch: 嫌疑人识得剑匣，也识得宝剑。
t_en: The suspect knew about the box and the sword.

t_ch: …我确实识得剑匣，也识得这柄魔剑。
t_en: ...Well, I do know about the box and the cursed sword.

t_ch: 不太可能是临时起意的偷盗。
t_en: The theft was planned in advance.

t_ch: …确实不像是见财起意。
t_en: ...Yeah, I don't think it was stolen on a whim.

t_ch: 正是如此，嫌疑人目的性很强，唯独偷走了孤云。
t_en: Exactly. The suspect had a clear motive and took only Guyun.

t_ch: 也就是说，除却参与赠剑仪式的你们之外，也只有使团相关人有机会接触这把剑。
t_en: So, besides the ceremony participants, only the delegation members had access to the sword.

t_ch: 有没有可能是使团偷了剑？
t_en: Could it be that someone from the delegation stole the sword?

t_ch: 呃…既然要偷走，使团又为何要把剑归还给仙舟啊？
t_en: Hmm... in that case, why would they return it to the Xianzhou in the first place?

t_ch: 也许使团后悔了？
t_en: Maybe they regretted their decision?

t_ch: 使团也不只一个人的。
t_en: Not everyone in the delegation has the same agenda.

t_ch: 你说的不无道理，当时的情况是，在那位纯美骑士的带领下，使团将剑送交工造司。负责交接的匠人证明，他们没有带走武库中的任何物品。
t_en: Hmm, that makes sense. The delegation, led by the Knight of Beauty, delivered the sword to the Artisanship Commission. The craftsman who received it testified that they didn't take anything from the arsenal.

t_ch: 有没有可能是银枝偷了剑？
t_en: Could it be that Argenti stole the sword?

t_ch: 银枝？那位纯美骑士啊…他和使团成员一道将剑移交给了工造司。
t_en: Argenti? Oh, that Knight of Beauty... He was with the delegation when they handed over the sword to the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 说个题外话，也许你不知道，正是这位骑士抵达卡勒瓦拉后，辨认出宝剑的来历，又一路护送它来到仙舟。换言之，是他一力促成了宝剑归乡之事。
t_en: You might not know, but he's the one who recognized the sword's origin on Kalevala and escorted it back to the Xianzhou. In other words, he's the one who made it possible for the sword to come home.

t_ch: 和使团一样，他要是想偷宝剑，何必非要在送来罗浮之后再将它偷走呢？
t_en: So, he wouldn't have gone through all that trouble to bring it to the Luofu if he wanted to keep the sword for himself, right?

t_ch: 有没有可能是匠人偷了剑？
t_en: Could it be that a craftsman stole the sword?

t_ch: 公输师傅检查了当班记录，把轮值的匠人与学徒都唤来我这儿问了一遍，并没有什么可疑的人物。
t_en: Master Gongshu checked the work records, and I questioned all the craftsmen and apprentices who were on shift at the time, but I didn't find any suspicious individuals.

t_ch: 有没有可能是我偷了剑？
t_en: Could it be that I stole the sword?

t_ch: 那你…偷了吗？
t_en: So... did you steal it?

t_ch: 我没偷。
t_en: No, I didn't.

t_ch: 那就别捣乱！
t_en: Then stop joking around!

t_ch: 我能证明，{NICKNAME}离开工造司后，来到了我这里，没有偷剑嫌疑。
t_en: I can prove that {NICKNAME} came to me after leaving the Artisanship Commission, so {F#she}{M#he} couldn't have stolen the sword.

t_ch: 简单来说，见过宝剑的人并非都去过工造司，想要宝剑的人并非都见过宝剑…这么排除下来，就只剩下云璃小姐一位嫌疑人了。
t_en: To put it simply, not everyone who's seen the sword has been to the Artisanship Commission, and not everyone who might want the sword has seen it... So, the only remaining suspect is Miss Yunli.

t_ch: 好有道理。
t_en: Fair enough.

t_ch: 那么……
t_en: So...

t_ch: 剑不是我偷的。
t_en: I didn't steal the sword.

t_ch: 哎呀，排除了各种可能之后，只留下了一种可能，那么它就是「真相」。
t_en: Well, when you have eliminated the impossible, whatever remains, however improbable, must be the truth.

t_ch: 不对，这还不是所有可能性。
t_en: No, this isn't the only remaining possibility.

t_ch: 我感觉，我们还有思维盲区。
t_en: I think we might be missing something here.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，要反驳我，得拿出像样的证据。
t_en: If you want to prove me wrong, {NICKNAME}, you'd better come up with some solid evidence.

t_ch: （整理一下思路，得帮助云璃摆脱嫌疑才行…）
t_en: (Let me gather my thoughts. I need to help Yunli clear her name...)

t_ch: （不知有没有遗漏什么线索…）
t_en: (Did we miss any clues?)

t_ch: 云璃说自己没有偷剑，我愿意信她。
t_en: Yunli says she didn't steal the sword, and I am willing to believe her.

t_ch: 但老朽也深知，她这般直来直往的性子最易酿成祸端。若她真的一时冲动窃走了孤云剑，我绝不会有一丝偏袒。
t_en: But I know well that Yunli can be impulsive, so if she did steal the sword on a whim, I won't show her any favors.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你昨日与云璃在一起，应该最有机会证明她的清白。你有什么想问我的吗？
t_en: You were with Yunli yesterday, {NICKNAME}, so you have the best chance to prove her innocence. Is there anything you need to ask me?

t_ch: 云璃后来去找你了吗？
t_en: Did she come to you afterward?

t_ch: 她还在与我赌气，没有找我。若当时与你一同回来，倒是能免了嫌疑。
t_en: No, she was still mad at me, so she didn't come by. If she had come with you, she wouldn't be a suspect right now.

t_ch: 保管宝剑的流程是怎样的？
t_en: How was the sword delivered?

t_ch: 赠剑仪式结束之后，由纯美骑士银枝携带剑匣，与使团一道，在云骑军护送下前往工造司，由工造司工正确认宝剑入库。
t_en: After the ceremony, Argenti carried the sword case, and together with the delegation, they were escorted by the Cloud Knights to the Artisanship Commission. The artisan there confirmed that the sword was safely stored.

t_ch: 我问得差不多了。
t_en: That's all I have to ask.

t_ch: 接下来，也要多麻烦你了。
t_en: I'm counting on you then.

t_ch: 我不想为难大毫执事官，我们就按照他的方式来证明清白吧。
t_en: I don't want to make things difficult for Official Dahao. Let's do this his way and prove my innocence.

t_ch: 我其实正在想，如果我找到了魔剑，是不是就算是直接洗脱了嫌疑？
t_en: If I can find that cursed sword, I can prove my innocence right away.

t_ch: 你这算盘打得太响了。
t_en: That would be too convenient.

t_ch: 如果能找到这把剑，就别怪我先下手为强了。
t_en: Well, don't blame me if I find it first and melt it down.

t_ch: 请问你要如何找到魔剑？
t_en: And how are you planning on finding the cursed sword?

t_ch: 这不正在想吗？
t_en: That's what I'm trying to figure out right now.

t_ch: 不，那叫「人赃并获」。
t_en: No, that would make it look like you did steal it.

t_ch: 还一件事我很在意，窃贼偷走了剑，却留下剑匣。难不成是像我一样，把剑熔了？
t_en: There's something else bothering me. Why would the thief take the sword but leave the case behind? Did they melt down the sword like I wanted to?

t_ch: 这很奇怪吗？
t_en: Is it really that strange?

t_ch: 带着剑堂而皇之地离开太惹眼了，对正常些的窃贼而言，带着剑匣离开不是更好吗。
t_en: I mean, carrying around the sword like that would be pretty conspicuous. Wouldn't a thief prefer to take the case as well?

t_ch: 也许是剑匣太费事了？
t_en: Maybe the case was too much trouble?

t_ch: 是啊。若是觉得剑匣费事，难道带着剑堂而皇之地离开就不惹眼吗？
t_en: Yeah, maybe. But if the case was too much trouble, wouldn't it be too conspicuous to carry around the sword like that in plain sight?

t_ch: 没想到，{RUBY_B#孤云}「米卡•奇瓦沙」{RUBY_E#}竟然被盗走了…嫌疑人竟还是怀炎将军的孙女？
t_en: Who could have thought that the {RUBY_B#Guyun}Miekka Kivessä{RUBY_E#} would be stolen... and that the prime suspect is General Huaiyan's granddaughter?

t_ch: 算了，不管怎样，使团的任务已经完成，接下来是仙舟自己的事情了。只是，那个女孩为何口口声声地称呼为魔剑？真是令人不快。
t_en: Regardless, we've accomplished our mission, and now it's the Xianzhou's problem. By the way, why did that girl call it a cursed sword? It's quite upsetting.

t_ch: 这把剑可能会夺人心智。
t_en: Because the sword can take over the wielder's mind.

t_ch: 这把剑里封存着妖物。
t_en: Because the sword contains an evil spirit.

t_ch: 够了！{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，我要严正声明，在卡勒瓦拉，这柄剑的地位向来都是无比崇高的！
t_en: That's enough, {F#young lady}{M#young man}! Let me get something straight: On Kalevala, this sword has always been highly revered!

t_ch: 这把剑的主人曾手执「米卡•奇瓦沙」为先民们斩妖除魔，而后又将宝剑交托给历代贤君。
t_en: The first master of the Miekka Kivessä slew countless demons and then passed it down to a line of wise monarchs.

t_ch: 君王们会听到寄宿于剑中的英雄之魂对他们进行教导，并帮助他们战胜强敌。
t_en: The monarchs would heed the guidance of the heroes' spirits within the sword and gain insight into defeating their enemies.

t_ch: 那是岁阳的声音？
t_en: Was that actually the voice of the heliobus?

t_ch: 那是…什么东西？哎，不管你说的究竟是什么……
t_en: What is... helio... what? Never mind...

t_ch: 传说故事中常有的桥段。
t_en: That's a common element in myths and legends.

t_ch: 当然…这确实可能只是神话。毕竟谁也没真的听过这把剑开口发声。
t_en: Of course... it could just be a myth. After all, no one has actually heard the sword speak.

t_ch: 「米卡•奇瓦沙」帮助过卡勒瓦拉先祖们创下基业，它绝不是什么「魔剑」！
t_en: The point is that the Miekka Kivessä did help the ancestors of Kalevala build a strong foundation! It's definitely not a "cursed sword"!

t_ch: 是了，你昨日发来消息后，老夫特地去武库查验了一番。
t_en: Yes, I received your message yesterday and went to the arsenal to check on it.

t_ch: 只是今天早上到工造司来一瞅，怎么就只剩剑匣了？除此之外，老夫什么都不知道，你问我也是白问。
t_en: But when I went back this morning, all that was left was the case. I don't know anything else, so asking more questions won't help, I'm afraid.

t_ch: 不过，作为工造司的工正，我必须说一句公道话——虽然这宝剑被盗走，但咱们这工造司还是很安全的！
t_en: However, as the master artisan of the Artisanship Commission, I've got to clarify something — even though the sword was stolen, this commission is still highly secure!

t_ch: 不对，老夫想起来了，我的宝贝金人也被人给砸坏了！唉呀！
t_en: Wait, no. I just remembered, my precious aurumatons were smashed!

t_ch: 星槎海码头的一角，你们找到了身着银甲的骑士。应该说，是他找上了你们。
t_en: In a corner of Starskiff Haven, you stumble upon a knight clad in shimmering silver armor. Or rather, it seems he has sought you out.

t_ch: 诸位，我已经在这里等候多时了。
t_en: I've been waiting for you here for quite a while.

t_ch: 窃贼偷走了剑，却留下剑匣，这可能是有原因的。
t_en: There must be a reason why the thief took the sword but left the case behind.

t_ch: 「米卡•奇瓦沙」帮助过卡勒瓦拉先祖们创下基业，它绝不是什么「魔剑」！
t_en: The point is that the Miekka Kivessä did help the ancestors of Kalevala build a strong foundation! It's definitely not a "cursed sword"!

t_ch: 老夫什么都不知道，你问我也是白问。
t_en: I don't know anything else, so asking more questions won't help.

t_ch: 用来封藏孤云的剑匣，它看上去很漂亮，这是它被盯上的原因吗？
t_en: The container used to seal Guyun is indeed a sight to behold. Its beauty is radiant. Perhaps it was this very beauty that has drawn unwanted attention.

t_ch: 无论如何，作为现场仅有被留下的物证之一，也许会留有窃贼的痕迹也说不准。
t_en: Regardless, as one of the few pieces of physical evidence left at the scene, it just might hold traces of the thief.

t_ch: 剑匣外壳
t_en: Sword Case Exterior

t_ch: 剑匣的外壳上以高妙的技艺嵌入金属丝，勾勒出了莲花纹样，似在昭示剑匣的产地：朱明仙舟。
t_en: The sword case, masterfully adorned with metal wire inlays, traces the pattern of lotus flowers, subtly proclaiming its origin from the illustrious Xianzhou Zhuming.

t_ch: 剑匣内鞘
t_en: Sword Case Interior

t_ch: 内鞘中铺设华贵的织物里衬，中心的凹槽恰好能放入一柄单手剑。
t_en: Inside the case, a luxurious fabric lining snugly cradles a recess designed to hold a single-handed sword, marrying opulence with precision.

t_ch: 剑匣锁扣
t_en: Sword Case Lock

t_ch: 藏于匣中的锁扣机关完好无损，并无被外力撬动、破坏的痕迹。
t_en: The locking mechanism nestled within the case remains intact, bearing no marks of external force or tampering.

t_ch: （也就是说…窃贼没有撬锁就取走了匣中的剑？）
t_en: (So... the thief took the sword from the case without picking the lock?)

t_ch: 打开剑匣
t_en: Open sword case

t_ch: 你察觉到内鞘底部似乎散落着几抹红色，它很难被人忽视——就像是刻意向查看内鞘的人宣布自己的存在一般。
t_en: You notice a few smears of red along the bottom of the sword case's interior. It's hard to ignore, as if a deliberate declaration of existence to those who are viewing the sword case.

t_ch: （将内鞘底部的红色取出来）
t_en: (Extract the red coloring from the interior of the sword case)

t_ch: 几片即将凋萎的花瓣，确切的说，玫瑰花瓣。
t_en: A few petals, teetering on the brink of withering. More precisely, a single rose petal.

t_ch: （为什么这片花瓣会出现在匣子里……？）
t_en: (Why is this flower petal inside the case...?)

t_ch: （合上剑匣）
t_en: (Close the sword case)

t_ch: 你合上剑匣，将它放回原处。
t_en: You close the sword case and put it back.

t_ch: 结束调查
t_en: End investigation

t_ch: 你合上剑匣，将它放回原处。
t_en: You close the sword case and put it back.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你要是找到什么线索，就当着大家的面说说。
t_en: If you've found any clues, {NICKNAME}, feel free to tell everyone.

t_ch: 云璃没有偷剑，我有十足把握。
t_en: I'm a hundred percent sure Yunli didn't steal the sword.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。虽然连我自己也没什么把握说服大毫先生……
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}, but I'm not sure if I can convince Mr. Dahao...

t_ch: 十足的把握？好啊，那我将我的推理过程再说一遍，要是有什么不对劲的地方，你可以随时打断。
t_en: A hundred percent sure? Fine, let me go through my reasoning again, and you can interrupt if anything feels off.

t_ch: 我想跟其他人再聊聊。
t_en: I'd like to talk to the others again.

t_ch: 嗯，你做好准备之后再来找我。
t_en: Alright, come back to me when you're ready.

t_ch: …刚才我已经将自己在卡勒瓦拉取出孤云时经历的一切告诉了各位，想必云璃小姐现在也像我那时一样，被剑中流淌着的纯美所感动。
t_en: ...I've told you everything about my experience with Master Guyun on Kalevala. And now, I'm sure that Miss Yunli is as moved by the sword's flawless beauty as I was back then.

t_ch: 老朽虽然听使团讲过这柄剑的传奇。但深入剑的记忆，其中的故事仍有一番离奇。云璃，我相信孤云的经历会让你有所改变。
t_en: Even though I had heard the legends of this sword from the delegation, delving into its memories was still fascinating. Yunli, I trust Guyun's experiences have changed your mind.

t_ch: 没想到这把剑有这样一段过去。
t_en: I had no idea that this sword had such a past.

t_ch: 直到握住孤云，我才会感受到它的所思所想，不能言说的一切。
t_en: It wasn't until I held Guyun in my hands that I could sense its thoughts and unspoken words.

t_ch: 怀炎将军说这柄剑会改变你。
t_en: General Huaiyan said this sword would change you.

t_ch: 所以爷爷才会瞒着我，是不想让我仓促地做出决定……
t_en: That's why Grandpa hid it from me, so I wouldn't make a hasty decision...

t_ch: 我原以为…被岁阳铸成的魔剑只会留下杀戮与愤怒，却没能想到还有人能留下了「结束战斗的渴望」，甚至改变了魔剑。
t_en: I always thought... a cursed sword infused with a heliobus would only bring rage and slaughter. I never thought someone could leave behind the desire to end war and even change the nature of the cursed sword.

t_ch: 是我太过偏激了。孤云毫无疑问是一柄英雄之剑，我…我为此前的冒昧向各位道歉。
t_en: My position was too extreme. Guyun is truly a Sword of Heroes. I... I apologize to all of you for my behavior.

t_ch: …对不起！
t_en: ...I'm sorry!

t_ch: 既然云璃姑娘洗清了嫌疑，宝剑也被找到了，现在也该是将它归还入库的时候了？
t_en: Now that we've proven Miss Yunli's innocence and found the sword, should we now return it to the arsenal?

t_ch: 不，我仍然要熔了这柄剑！
t_en: No, I'm still going to melt down this sword!

t_ch: 哎，云璃啊云璃……
t_en: Oh, Yunli...

t_ch: 云璃找了一处无人打扰的清静地方，将她与魔剑之间的纠葛和盘托出。
t_en: Yunli finds a quiet place where no one would disturb her and lays out the entanglement between her and the cursed sword.

t_ch: 骑士明白骑士决斗的规矩，他不再争辩，而是满心期待着你和云璃能够倾听孤云的过去。
t_en: The knight understands the rules of knightly duels. He ceases arguing and instead eagerly awaits for you and Yunli to listen to Guyun's past.

t_ch: 某月十五日 下午 4:<unbreak>04</unbreak>
t_en: <unbreak>15</unbreak>th day of Month X, <unbreak>16</unbreak>:<unbreak>04</unbreak>

t_ch: 朱明制式的剑匣，用来封藏孤云。它的锁扣完好无损，被轻轻打开；内鞘底部留下几片玫瑰花瓣，略显刻意。
t_en: A sword casket produced by the Zhuming that is used to seal Guyun. Its opened lock appears to have not been damaged. At the bottom of the casket, a few rose petals have been intentionally left behind.

t_ch: 说服公司代表斯科特
t_en: Convince IPC Rep, Skott

t_ch: 与飞霄谈判
t_en: Negotiate with Feixiao

t_ch: 与斯科特谈判
t_en: Negotiate with Skott

t_ch: …我的脑袋一团乱麻，且容我想想——
t_en: My thoughts are a mess... Let me sort them out...

t_ch: 剑匣并未上锁，显然只有将剑送来的人才能办到；而玫瑰花瓣，则是那位纯美骑士留下的。
t_en: The sword case was never locked, which is evidence that only someone who delivered the sword could have done that. And the rose petal was probably left behind by that Knight of Beauty.

t_ch: 但他到底是如何将剑带离工造司的？又为什么要在送还宝剑后将它偷走？还有，他为什么要在匣中留下这片花瓣？
t_en: But how did he manage to take the sword from the Artisanship Commission? Why did he steal it after returning it here? And why would he leave that petal in the case?

t_ch: 这我哪知道啊？
t_en: How am I supposed to know?

t_ch: 你问问银枝，别问我。
t_en: Ask Argenti, not me.

t_ch: 我知道。
t_en: I know.

t_ch: 是孤云剑自己脱离了剑匣。那名纯美骑士所做的，只是打开锁扣，帮助它逃走。
t_en: Guyun flew out of the case on its own. The Knight of Beauty only unlocked the box to aid its escape.

t_ch: 别开玩笑了，剑怎么可能自己逃走？
t_en: Don't be ridiculous. How can a sword escape from a case on its own?

t_ch: 怎么，你是没见过飞剑吗？
t_en: You've never seen a Flying Sword before?

t_ch: …我、我只在传说中听过「米卡•奇瓦沙」会飞，那退一万步讲，那位纯美骑士为什么要这么做？
t_en: I... I've only heard about the Miekka Kivessä being able to fly in legends. But even if it could fly, why would the Knight of Beauty do this?

t_ch: 我早说过了，那是一柄铸入岁阳的魔剑，一定是这把剑控制了银枝！也许在抵达罗浮之前，他就被控制了。
t_en: Like I said, the sword contains a heliobus. Argenti must have been under the control of the sword! Maybe he was under its control even before you arrived on the Luofu.

t_ch: 大毫执事官，骑士先生在哪？
t_en: Official Dahao, where can we find that knight?

t_ch: 呃，让我找找…银枝登记的星船「希世难得号」的停泊位置……
t_en: Um... let me see... the docking location of Argenti's ship, the One and Only...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们走！在那把剑完全夺取银枝心智前，要尽快找到他！
t_en: Let's go, {NICKNAME}! We've got to find Argenti before that sword completely takes over his mind!

t_ch: 纯美骑士，就是你偷走了孤云剑对吧？把它留下！你根本不知道那魔剑是有多危险。
t_en: Knight of Beauty, you're the one who stole Guyun, right? Hand it over! You have no idea how dangerous that cursed sword is.

t_ch: 危险？依我的愚见，真正危险的永远是挥动武器的手。
t_en: Dangerous? In my humble opinion, the real danger is always the hand wielding the weapon.

t_ch: 为什么现在要偷走孤云？
t_en: Why steal Guyun now?

t_ch: 容我纠正您的说法：我从未试图偷窃这件武器。身为伊德莉拉的骑士，我誓言安贫乐道，绝不占有超出必要的财物。
t_en: Please allow me to correct your statement: I never attempted to steal this weapon. As Idrila's knight, I've sworn to live in poverty and possess only what is necessary.

t_ch: 所以，玫瑰是你故意留下的？
t_en: So, you intentionally left the rose petal behind?

t_ch: 正是。身为伊德莉拉的骑士，我誓言安贫乐道，对偷窃行为有所不齿——为了兼顾对孤云阁下的承诺以及骑士誓言，便只好留下玫瑰。
t_en: Yes, I did. As Idrila's knight, I've sworn to live in poverty, and I abhor theft. I had to leave behind a rose petal in order to keep my promise to Master Guyun and uphold my knightly oath.

t_ch: 什、什么意思？
t_en: Wh—What do you mean?

t_ch: 我答应了帮助孤云阁下的请求，它希望我能带它逃离仙舟，作为将它拔出石碑的人，被它的纯美所打动的人，我不能辜负它。
t_en: I agreed to assist Master Guyun in its escape from the Xianzhou. As the person who pulled it from the stone monument, captivated by its beauty, I must not let it down.

t_ch: 我既不能留在武库中说服工匠，也不愿意如同窃贼一般带走宝剑。为此我留下了一个代表我身份的小小证据，希望能与各位开诚布公地谈一谈。
t_en: I couldn't stay in the arsenal to convince the craftsmen, nor did I want to take the sword like a thief. That's why I left behind a small piece of evidence that pointed to my identity in the hope of an open and honest conversation with you.

t_ch: 说一千道一万，你还是要带着魔剑离开仙舟，把剑给我！
t_en: Whatever your reasons are, you're planning to take the cursed sword away from the Xianzhou! Hand it over to me!

t_ch: 当初是孤云阁下的纯美打动了我，我才心甘情愿随旅团奔赴归剑之旅…但遗憾的是，孤云阁下的故乡并非它的理想归处，纯美骑士理应善始善终。
t_en: Back then, it was the beauty of Master Guyun that impressed me, so I willingly embarked on the journey to return the sword... But unfortunately, Master Guyun's homeland is no longer the ideal place for it to return to. As a Knight of Beauty, I must not abandon it halfway.

t_ch: 不行，他已经完全被魔剑的力量诱惑了。
t_en: Oh, no. He's completely captivated by the power of that cursed sword.

t_ch: 要怎么才能让他清醒过来。
t_en: How can we snap him out of it?

t_ch: 方法，就在你的手上。
t_en: The solution is right in your hand.

t_ch: 简直就像是会洗脑的恶魔。
t_en: It's like a devil that can mess with your mind.

t_ch: 这才是魔剑的可怕之处。
t_en: That's what makes cursed swords so scary.

t_ch: 诱惑？在下被孤云阁下的纯美所动容了！
t_en: Captivated? No, I am touched by Master Guyun's flawless beauty!

t_ch: 在下反倒是疑惑，在场的诸位竟然没有一人能够读懂这柄剑，真是令人感到悲伤…我已经下定决心，要带着孤云阁下离开仙舟。
t_en: To be honest, I'm confused why none of you can understand this sword. This is quite heartrending... I've already made up my mind to take Master Guyun away from the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 魔剑已经影响了你的认知，我会帮你摆脱它！我会亲手打碎这个幻象——还有这把剑！
t_en: That cursed sword has messed with your ability to think, but I'll break you free from its grip! I'll shatter its illusion... and the sword itself!

t_ch: 美丽的小姐，若你执意要夺走受我保护之人…之物，请和我进行一场符合骑士礼仪的公平对决。
t_en: Fair lady, if you're intent on taking away this person... this sword that is under my protection, please engage me with a fair duel according to knightly etiquette.

t_ch: 作为骑士决斗代理人，银枝，向您发起挑战！
t_en: As a representative in this duel, I, Argenti, challenge you!

t_ch: 代理决斗？
t_en: A representative?

t_ch: 我以骑士精神宣誓，在纯美的女神伊德莉拉的见证下，我将成为孤云阁下的决斗代理人。
t_en: I swear on the spirit of chivalry, in front of Idrila the Beauty, that I will represent Master Guyun in this duel.

t_ch: 若我取得胜利，我希望孤云阁下能随我一同离开仙舟；若云璃小姐取得胜利，我会坦然接受失败，并将孤云阁下双手奉还——
t_en: If I win, you will allow Master Guyun to leave the Xianzhou with me. And if you best me, Miss Yunli, I'll accept my defeat and turn Master Guyun over to you...

t_ch: 云璃小姐，您是否接受挑战？
t_en: Do you accept my challenge, Miss Yunli?

t_ch: 就让我瞧瞧，你带走它的渴望，和我狩猎魔剑的愿望，究竟哪个更胜一筹！
t_en: Fine, let's see whose desire is stronger, your desire to take it away, or my desire to hunt down all cursed swords!

t_ch: 云璃，前来讨教！
t_en: Show me what you've got!

t_ch: 你不打算使用孤云与我一决胜负？
t_en: You're not going to use Guyun to fight for victory?

t_ch: 在下虽略懂剑术，但在如此庄严的决斗中，骑士理应使用日夜锤炼的技艺前来应战。
t_en: While I know a thing or two about swordplay, in a proper duel like this, a knight should rely on the skills he's honed day and night.

t_ch: 真是怪人一个。
t_en: You're a real weirdo.

t_ch: 我输了。按照骑士决斗的规矩，我理应将孤云剑双手奉上。
t_en: I've lost. According to the rules of a knight's duel, I should keep my promise and hand over the sword to you.

t_ch: 但在此之前，我仍希望身为胜者的您，有雅量能倾听孤云阁下过去的经历……
t_en: But before I do that, I hope that you, as the winner, might have mercy and listen to Master Guyun's past experiences...

t_ch: 从被锻成那日起，这柄剑就已坠入魔道。现在，把剑放在我面前，让我击碎它！
t_en: This sword has been cursed since the day it was forged. Place it in front of me, and let me destroy it!

t_ch: 骑士，就按她说的做吧。
t_en: Do what she says, Knight.

t_ch: 但是——
t_en: But...

t_ch: 你为我已做了很多。现在该是我独自面对她的时候了。
t_en: You've done so much for me already. It's time for me to face her alone.

t_ch: 银枝取出伤痕累累的古剑。在所有人的视线中，剑缓缓升起，锋刃上似有火光舞动。
t_en: Argenti draws forth an ancient sword, scarred from countless battles. Before all eyes, the blade rises slowly, its edge dancing with the semblance of flames.

t_ch: 各位，我厌倦了战斗。我只想向你们讲述我的过去，讲述我为何想要逃离。
t_en: I'm weary of fighting. I just want to share my past with you, to explain why I wanted to escape.

t_ch: 在此之后，我的命运将由你们决定。小姑娘，你害怕见证我的过去吗？
t_en: After that, I'll leave my destiny in your hands. Are you afraid to witness my past, little girl?

t_ch: 害怕？你确实与其他魔剑有着不一样的气质，但这不过是为了利用好奇心来蛊惑我罢了。
t_en: Afraid? Well, you do have a different aura from other cursed swords, but you're just trying to use my curiosity to lure me in.

t_ch: 那你最是清楚，仅是一次触碰，我不会夺走任何人的理智。
t_en: Well, you know best that a single touch won't snatch away anyone's sanity.

t_ch: 云璃，千万小心。
t_en: Just be careful, Yunli.

t_ch: 云璃，我认为可以一试。
t_en: I think it's worth a shot, Yunli.

t_ch: 好啊，就让我瞧瞧。
t_en: Fine, show me your past.

t_ch: 跟着我来吧。
t_en: Follow me.

t_ch: 这里是…你的记忆？
t_en: Is this... your memory?

t_ch: 好冷啊～明明战斗的时候没有体会，现在真是冻到骨头里了。
t_en: It's so cold here... I didn't feel it when I was in the thick of battle, but now it's freezing me to the bone.

t_ch: 真不愧是朱明仙舟的杰作。要不是你刚才接管了我的手，我恐怕会在刚才的战斗中身首异处了……
t_en: You're indeed a masterpiece from the Xianzhou Zhuming. If you hadn't taken control of my body, I probably would've been decapitated in that fight...

t_ch: 闭嘴，保持警惕！步离狼卒的狩猎永不休止。
t_en: Quiet! And stay alert! Borisin Wolftroopers never give up the hunt.

t_ch: 别这么严肃，聊两句嘛。距离咱们俩流落在这个世界快三百多个自转日了。发信器从来也没个响，看来联络大部队是指望不上了。
t_en: Hey, just relax a bit. It's been over <unbreak>300</unbreak> days since we were both stranded on this planet. The transmitter never rings, so I guess we can't count on that anymore.

t_ch: 这个世界的居民还在用驼兽拉车，让他们帮忙修好星槎显然不可能。
t_en: The people on this planet still use beasts to pull their carts. Obviously, there's no way they can help us fix the starskiff.

t_ch: 咱们俩的余生都要被锁死在这颗星球上了。
t_en: Looks like we're stranded on this forsaken planet for the rest of our lives.

t_ch: 余生，你们仙舟人的余生可太长了。感到绝望了吗？一个人，面对一个充满敌意的世界。
t_en: The rest of a Xianzhou native's life could be terribly long. Are you feeling any desperation? All by yourself in this hostile world?

t_ch: 有一点。
t_en: Yeah, a bit, perhaps.

t_ch: 如果熬不下去的话，可以把一切都交给我。你不必思考那些过于沉重的东西……
t_en: When things get too rough for you to handle, just leave everything to me. No need to carry the weight of the world on your shoulders...

t_ch: 好啊，如果真的熬不住了，我会把身体交给你支配的。
t_en: Yeah, sure. If I really can't handle things anymore, I'll leave my body to you.

t_ch: 不过眼下还不是时候。
t_en: But now is not that time.

t_ch: 凭你那样低微的本事，能反抗一整支步离人的军队吗？你到底在坚持什么啊？
t_en: Do you really think you can oppose an entire borisin army with your limited skills? What are you holding out for?

t_ch: 我被困在了这里，但这支步离人猎群应该也遇到了同样的困境……
t_en: It's true that I'm stranded here, but it seems like that borisin pack is in the same boat...

t_ch: 对了孤云，你知道「种子」吗？种子，是微尘般不起眼的东西。但只要细心栽培，再假以时日，它却能长成不可撼动的参天大树。
t_en: By the way, Guyun, do you know what a seed is? A seed may seem insignificant, but with careful cultivation and time, it can grow into a huge unwavering tree.

t_ch: 你说「仙舟人余生太长了」…没错，我打算用接下来的岁月培育一枚种子，名为「反抗」的种子，我要让这片土地的人们懂得拿起武器去反抗那些怪物。
t_en: You said that the rest of a Xianzhou native's life could be terribly long... You're right. That's why I intend to plant a seed named "resistance" here and nurture it for the rest of my life. I want to show the people of this land how to defend themselves against those monsters.

t_ch: 我已经…在这些人眼中看到了这种子。
t_en: I can already see the seed in the eyes of those people.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，你能做到吗？不如把身体交给我，杀一个够本，杀两个赚翻。
t_en: Hehe, you really think you can do it? Why not just give me control, and we can revel in the bloodbath together?

t_ch: 刚才的记忆是？
t_en: What was the memory I just saw?

t_ch: 那是我和一名云骑流落异星的记忆。
t_en: It was a memory of me and a Cloud Knight, stranded on an unfamiliar planet.

t_ch: 我和他坠落在了一颗被步离人占据的星球。只有…我们。
t_en: We crash-landed on a planet occupied by borisin. Just the two of us.

t_ch: 我能感受到，这是一场孤独的战争。
t_en: I can feel that it was a lonely war.

t_ch: 我还活着，可村子里那些年轻人……
t_en: I'm alive, but all the young people in the village...

t_ch: 他们死了，为你而死，为你承诺的「没有步离人的好日子」而死。
t_en: They died. They died for you. They died because of your big promises, promising them a life free from the borisin.

t_ch: 我品尝到了他们临死前的绝望。他们看不见这个日子到来了。你也一样。
t_en: I tasted their despair before death. They didn't see it coming, and neither will you.

t_ch: 我们…不能在这里停留了。去下一个村子吧。
t_en: We can't stay here... Let's move on to the next village.

t_ch: 为什么？你还在坚持什么？！你和你那支可怜的杂牌军已经打了三十多年仗了。你们从没赢过。你播种的根本不是「反抗」，只是「绝望」罢了。
t_en: Why? What's the point!? You and your sorry excuse for an army have been fighting for over three decades, and you haven't won a single battle! What you're sowing is not "resistance," but "despair."

t_ch: 这个世界已经无药可救了，它被步离人的猎群感染了！
t_en: This world is hopeless! It's completely infested with packs of borisin!

t_ch: 你看到过他们的数量，你也知道他们的手段…而你寄予希望的那些野人，他们只知道匍匐在地上，恭敬地称呼那些吃掉他们子女和亲人的怪物为「兽主」！
t_en: You've seen their numbers, and you know their methods... And those commoners you've put your hopes in, they just kneel down and worship the monsters devouring their children and families, calling them their Beastmasters!

t_ch: 你为他们斩下兽主的头颅，他们却害怕遭到报复，把你供了出来，这些事情，你全都记得的吧！
t_en: You beheaded one of the Beastmasters for their sake, but they turned on you because they were scared of the borisin taking revenge. You remember all that, right!?

t_ch: 我…记得！我记得我的第一次死亡。步离人…把我的头切了下来。
t_en: I... I do! I remember my first death. The borisin... cut off my head.

t_ch: 但我也记得，如果不是有个年轻人为了收葬我，冒险将它缝了回去，我已经彻底入灭了。
t_en: But I also remember that if it weren't for a brave youngster who risked his neck to reattach my head and give me a proper burial, I would've disappeared completely.

t_ch: 我记得这些人为我做的一切：放在山洞口的食物，缝补的皮袄，还有那些人加入我之前立下的誓言。
t_en: I remember everything the people did for me: They left food at the entrance of my cave, fixed my fur coat, and some even joined my resistance.

t_ch: 仙舟人的余生还很长，会有新的年轻人站出来和我一起战斗的！时间站在我们这边。
t_en: The rest of this Xianzhou native's life is still terribly long, and there will be new youngsters who will stand beside me in battle! Time is on our side.

t_ch: 你刚才说，这是一场孤独的战争。
t_en: You said it was a lonely war.

t_ch: 不，这场战争并不孤独，只是充满了绝望。
t_en: No, it wasn't a war filled with loneliness, but with despair.

t_ch: 多到我不愿再品尝绝望的滋味。
t_en: So much despair that I don't ever want to taste it again.

t_ch: 我要走了。
t_en: I'm leaving.

t_ch: 您要去哪儿，大人？
t_en: Where are you going, Your Highness?

t_ch: 我要…回故乡去了。回到天空之上，很远很远的地方。我不会再回来了。
t_en: I'm returning... to my homeland, a distant place beyond the sky. I'm not coming back.

t_ch: 可是…您要是走了，我们该怎么办？万一那些狼妖再度出现……
t_en: But... what should we do with you gone? What if those wolf demons show up again?

t_ch: 他们已经死绝了，孩子。他们已经被你们尽数铲除了……
t_en: They won't, my child. You've wiped out the last of them...

t_ch: 在我离开前，我要求你们为我做一件事。虽然狼妖不在了，但世间依旧还会有怪物。
t_en: But before I go, I need you to do one thing for me. Even though the wolf demons are gone, new monsters will appear in this world.

t_ch: 过不了多久，会有身披金色枝条，只知杀戮的妖魔在此徘徊。但是你们不必害怕。
t_en: Soon, a demon will appear, donning golden branches and knowing only bloodshed. However, fear not, my children.

t_ch: 我将这柄追随我征战多年的剑留给你们。用我这些年所教方法，你们必能将这世间最后一只怪物斩杀。
t_en: I'm entrusting you with this sword that's been with me in countless battles. Use the skills I've taught you over the years, and you'll surely defeat that last demon.

t_ch: 但是，我要你们为我建立一座石碑。一定要够大，够坚固，要将那怪物的遗骸牢牢压住，万世不移。这是你们欠我的债，你们必须做到，明白吗？
t_en: But that's not all. I need you to construct a stone monument for me. It must be large and sturdy enough to seal the remains of that demon forever. This is a debt you owe me, and you must fulfill it. Do you understand?

t_ch: 我们…明白。
t_en: We... understand.

t_ch: 你骗了他们，也骗了我。
t_en: You lied to them, and you lied to me, too.

t_ch: 哦？
t_en: Oh?

t_ch: 你答应在你绝望的时候，会将身体交给我的。
t_en: You promised that you'd give your body to me when you couldn't handle things anymore.

t_ch: 老朋友，仙舟人的余生现在到尽头了。
t_en: Well, this Xianzhou native's life has finally come to an end, my old friend.

t_ch: 那就给你个报仇的机会，来送我最后一程吧？
t_en: How about one last chance for revenge? Come and bid me farewell for the final time.

t_ch: …他早已经做好了觉悟，我能感觉到无数复杂的感受在流淌。
t_en: ...He had long since come to some understanding. I could sense a mix of intricate emotions flowing out.

t_ch: 这并不是嗜血、嗔怒、畏怖的情绪，他为你留下的是…寄托？
t_en: It wasn't bloodlust, or anger, or fear. What he left for you was... hope.

t_ch: 「他们已知晓如何反抗怪物，而世间仍有最后一头怪物在徘徊。除掉它，我的老友。」
t_en: "They've learned how to fight monsters, but there's still one last monster lingering in the world. Kill it, my old friend."

t_ch: 他将我留下，不再回头。
t_en: He left me there without ever looking back.

t_ch: …谢谢你，老友。
t_en: ...Thank you, my old friend.

t_ch: 岁阳能品尝人类的情绪……
t_en: Heliobi can taste human emotions...

t_ch: 我的…情绪是什么味道呢，老友……？
t_en: How do my emotions taste... my old friend...?

t_ch: 一点点苦涩的怜悯，一点点滚烫的勇气，你心底最大的渴望是结束战斗。
t_en: A hint of bitter compassion and a touch of scalding courage. Your heart's deepest desire is to end war once and for all.

t_ch: 现在，战斗结束了…你听见了吗？
t_en: Now, the war is finally over... Can you hear?

t_ch: 虽然你已听不见了，老友，但我会尽我所能，结束这个世界的战斗。
t_en: Even though you can't hear me anymore, my old friend, I'll do everything I can to bring an end to all conflicts in the world.

t_ch: 所以，这就是卡勒瓦拉人所说的英雄之剑的过去？你帮助那个世界的人类结束了战斗？
t_en: So, this is the past of the Kalevalan people's Sword of Heroes? You helped the people of that world bring an end to war?

t_ch: 不，我没能做到。身为一柄武器，我只会成为下一场争端的开始。
t_en: No, I did not. I am a weapon, and I only became the cause of more conflicts.

t_ch: 卡勒瓦拉人的历代君王视我为天赋王权的标志，他们你争我夺，为了得到我发动战争。
t_en: Countless monarchs of Kalevala saw me as a symbol of kingship endowed by nature, and they waged wars in order to possess me.

t_ch: 我受够了那些人龌龊的念头，我也不愿再为任何人行杀戮之事。
t_en: I grew tired of their wicked intentions. I didn't want to kill for anyone anymore.

t_ch: 于是我回到了埋葬那家伙的石碑前——从此之后，再也无人能将我从石碑中拔出。
t_en: So, I returned to the stone monument where my master was buried, and since then, no one was able to remove me from it.

t_ch: …可是，你却让银枝将你取出？
t_en: ...But you allowed Argenti to take you away?

t_ch: 因为即便过去了这么久…我依然想让那人回到故乡。
t_en: Because even after all this time... I still wanted my master to come back home.

t_ch: 在赠剑仪式上，我得知自己会被赠给最好的剑士重新陷阵杀敌时，我感到了痛苦。
t_en: During the sword-gifting ceremony, I felt anguish when I learned that I would be handed over to the most skilled swordmaster and once again become a tool for killing.

t_ch: 那人将自己最深的渴望：「结束战斗」，铭刻进了我的剑身。
t_en: My master had engraved his deepest desire into my blade: End war.

t_ch: 身为被锻造之物，我的功能从一开始就已被注定，我必须成为一把武器，不断砍杀。
t_en: As a weapon, my purpose was predetermined from the start: I had to become a deadly sword that would relentlessly hack and kill.

t_ch: 可是当我的使命完成时，我是否能像那个人一样，拥有选择自己命运的权利呢？因此，我请求纯美骑士帮助我离开，不必再陷入杀戮和战斗的轮回。
t_en: But once I fulfilled my mission, would I have the right to choose my own destiny like he did? So, I asked the Knight of Beauty to help me break free from this never-ending cycle of bloodshed.

t_ch: 这千百年的时光，在一瞬之间成为了我理所当然的回忆。
t_en: These thousands of years have naturally turned into memories of mine.

t_ch: …我好像，我好像明白了那位骑士所说的「纯美」究竟为何了。
t_en: ...I think I understand the "beauty" the Knight was talking about now.

t_ch: 我们接下来，会去往下一个记忆的片段，出发吗？
t_en: Shall we proceed to the next fragment of my memories?

t_ch: 出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 等等，别着急。
t_en: Let's not rush.

t_ch: 我会等你。
t_en: I'll be waiting for you.

t_ch: 我倾听到了你真正的愿望，孤云。我感受到你已经厌倦了继续成为一柄剑，落入不知何人之手，进行你所不愿的战斗，难道不是吗？
t_en: I've heard your true desires, Guyun. I can sense that you're tired of being a sword, constantly serving new masters and fighting battles you no longer want to be a part of, right?

t_ch: 这是作为剑与生俱来的命运，我注定会落入某人之手，进行战斗。风能选择停止吹拂吗？云能选择停止漂泊吗？不能，这便是受造之物的命运。
t_en: But being a sword is my destiny. I'm doomed to serve some master and fight battles endlessly. Can the wind choose to stop blowing? Can a cloud choose to stop drifting? No, it's the same for me, a creation with a predetermined destiny.

t_ch: 不，你见识过无名的剑士如何决定自己的命运，孤云，当然你也可以。
t_en: That's not true, Guyun. That Nameless Swordmaster shaped his own destiny, so you can too.

t_ch: 我实在想不到，我作为剑以外的命运……
t_en: I just can't picture my destiny being anything other than that of a sword...

t_ch: 你记得吗？你在剑士的石碑前孤高的伫立，人们望向你，是因为你承载着对英雄的追忆。于是，那时的你就不再是一柄剑了，而是一块纪念碑！
t_en: Do you remember when you stood alone in front of the Swordmaster's stone monument? People admired you because you carried the memories of the hero. You were no longer a sword. You were a memorial.

t_ch: …后来的我斑驳不堪，轻风从剑身上的空洞穿过，还会发出高低不同的音调，去向远方的山野——原来那时的我是乐器，我怀念作为乐器的时光。
t_en: ...Later, as I became worn and weathered, the gentle wind would pass through the hollows of my body, creating melodic tones that echoed in the distant mountains. I became a musical instrument then. I miss those times.

t_ch: 受造之物是否有资格成为自己想成为的样子？当然可以！我会实现孤云你的愿望，我会将你铸造成另一种器物。
t_en: Can a creation become what it wants to be? The answer is "yes"! I'll grant your wish, Guyun, and transform you into something different.

t_ch: …另一种器物？
t_en: ...Something different?

t_ch: 可是，这是演武仪典的奖品，卡勒瓦拉的使团要是知道了…怀炎将军，将军您快说句话啊？
t_en: But this is the prize for the Wardance... If the Kalevalan delegation finds out... Please say something, General Huaiyan!

t_ch: 唉，老朽这个孙女，性格向来执拗。云璃，你真的确定要这么做吗？
t_en: Well, my granddaughter has always been stubborn. Are you really sure about this, Yunli?

t_ch: 大家…觉得呢？
t_en: What do you all think?

t_ch: 如果是这样，我会同意。
t_en: If that's what you want, I'm on board.

t_ch: 不妨问问孤云的意见。
t_en: You might as well ask Guyun's opinion.

t_ch: 完成孤云阁下的意愿，正是我抵达仙舟的目的。伊德莉拉在上，无论是铸剑为犁，或是其他，只要能让孤云阁下结束战斗，我都双手赞成！
t_en: Fulfilling Master Guyun's wishes is the reason I came to the Xianzhou. With Idrila's blessing, you could turn Master Guyun into a plow, but as long as it fulfills his wish to end war, I support it fully!

t_ch: 那就拜托你了，云璃。
t_en: Then I'll leave it to you, Yunli.

t_ch: 最后，你们前往工造司的五兵坊，借用其中的铸炉。
t_en: Finally, you make your way to the Artisanship Commission's Pentad Armory, seeking the use of their forging furnace.

t_ch: 你在难过个什么劲啊，小丫头。是感到后悔了吗？
t_en: Why the long face, my child? Are you feeling regret, perhaps?

t_ch: 熔剑的时候你可比谁都斩钉截铁，怎么现在反倒一副心不甘情不愿的样子啊？
t_en: You were completely determined when melting down the sword, so why the sudden change of heart?

t_ch: 爷爷，我熔掉了演武仪典的奖品。你该怎么向罗浮交代啊？
t_en: Grandpa, I melted down the prize for the Wardance. How are you going to explain this to the Luofu?

t_ch: 好家伙，这么小的云璃身上居然长了这么大个良心。呵呵，不容易啊不容易啊。
t_en: Oh, am I hearing right? This little girl is worried about her grandpa! Hehe, didn't see that coming.

t_ch: 啧。
t_en: Tsk.

t_ch: 唉，如果一开始我能冷静地找爷爷谈谈…也许这件事不会闹出如今这样的轩然大波。
t_en: If only I had talked to you from the beginning, Grandpa... Maybe things wouldn't have escalated like this.

t_ch: 不过，孤云剑能得偿所愿，也是件好事。关于这点我倒是不后悔。
t_en: But at least Guyun got what it wanted. I have no regrets there.

t_ch: 云璃…你和你父亲，至少在这件事上还是有点像的。
t_en: When it comes to swords, you... you and your father are cut from the same cloth.

t_ch: 含光所造的其余武器，你还打算继续回收吗？
t_en: So, are you planning to continue melting down the rest of Hanguang's swords?

t_ch: 嗯。虽然孤云有些特别，但对于大多数持剑者来说，岁阳魔剑还是太危险了。
t_en: Yeah, Guyun is unique, but the other cursed swords are still too dangerous for anyone to handle.

t_ch: 只是在下次冲动之前，我会先等一等。
t_en: But maybe I'll try to be more patient next time.

t_ch: 好个「等一等」。记住今天，在将来的日子里，还会有许多需要我们「等一等」的事情出现。
t_en: "Be more patient." Well said. Remember what you learned today, because there will be many more situations in the future that will require you to "be more patient."

t_ch: 明白了。可是，奖品怎么办？
t_en: Understood, but what about the prize?

t_ch: 打从你在赠剑仪式上出现，爷爷心中就警铃大作。为此我特地准备了另一把剑作为替代。放心，剑里绝对没有添加岁阳！
t_en: Well, ever since you showed up at the ceremony, I had a hunch, so I prepared another sword as a backup plan. And don't worry, this one has nothing to do with heliobi!

t_ch: 那可真是太好了！
t_en: That's perfect!

t_ch: 对了，卡勒瓦拉的使节和那位骑士一同去了金人巷。他们打算挑一处地方纪念英雄。咱们一起去瞧瞧吧。
t_en: By the way, the ambassador from Kalevala and that knight went to Aurum Alley. They're looking for a spot to honor the hero. Why don't we go take a look together?

t_ch: 我们走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 啊，怀炎将军怎么有空前来观礼？
t_en: Oh, General Huaiyan! What brings you here?

t_ch: 我的孙女在听闻「米卡•奇瓦沙」讲述的英雄传说后，备受鼓舞，特地要求我带着她来这儿看看。
t_en: Well, my granddaughter was inspired by the heroic legends told by the Miekka Kivessä, so she dragged me here to take a look.

t_ch: 这株金色的树苗是…？
t_en: What's this golden sapling...?

t_ch: 为何选在这里纪念？
t_en: Why did you choose this place?

t_ch: 我是卡勒瓦拉守碑人的后裔。在下的家族世世代代负责看护着那位无名英雄留下的巨大石碑。
t_en: I come from a long line of Kalevalan monument keepers. For generations, my family has guarded the stone monument left behind by the nameless hero.

t_ch: 数百年来，石碑底下生长出一株金色的树，枝条从未枯朽。卡勒瓦拉人视它为神话存在的证明。
t_en: Over the centuries, a golden tree sprouted from under the monument, and its branches have never withered. We Kalevalans see it as proof of the hero's existence.

t_ch: 在出发前，我从这株金树上折下一枝，希望能将它移栽至此，作为英雄回归故乡的见证。
t_en: Before leaving Kalevala, I took a branch from that golden tree, hoping to plant it here to represent the hero returning to his homeland.

t_ch: 甚好。仙舟人不设棺椁和灵位，能在故土上人来人往、烟火热闹之处安眠，想必他一定很满意。
t_en: That sounds good. We Xianzhou people don't make coffins or set up memorials, so I'm sure he would be happy to rest here where life is bustling.

t_ch: 英雄曾要求先民们在石碑上刻下一些文字。此次出访，我也将文字拓了下来。
t_en: The hero had asked our ancestors to carve something into the monument, so I made a copy of it too.

t_ch: 虽然岁月侵蚀了碑文，但它看起来似乎还能辨认——
t_en: While the inscription is pretty worn with age, perhaps you can still make out some of it...

t_ch: 「飘零他乡，身殒在即，幸不辱天职。」
t_en: "I have drifted far from my homeland and am on the brink of death. Yet, I take solace in having honorably fulfilled my duties."

t_ch: 「若有过客见怜，请携碣前黄土一抔，归葬罗浮。」
t_en: "Should you feel compassion for me, kind traveler, please take a handful of soil hence and bring it back to the Luofu."

t_ch: 帕沃尔先生，不知这位英雄是否在你们的历史上留下过名讳？
t_en: Mr. Paavo, I wonder if that hero ever left a name in your history?

t_ch: 抱歉，那位英雄战斗的日子已经离我们太远太远了，他没留下过任何可供旁人称呼的名字。但在我们的神话里，他从天上来到大地，我们称他为云骑士。
t_en: I apologize, but the hero's epic battles happened long ago, and he did not leave any name for us to call him. But in our mythology, he came from the heavens, and so we call him the Cloud Knight.

t_ch: 欢迎回家，云骑士。
t_en: Welcome home, Cloud Knight.

t_ch: 你也可以在这儿好好休息了，孤云。
t_en: Now you can also rest here, Guyun.
